title: (54) an absorbent core for a baby diaper
abstract: The invention relates to an absorbent core for a baby diaper and finds application as a disposable hygiene product for babies and small children. According to the proposed solution, the baby diaper absorbent core consists of a mixture of cellulose and superabsorbent, wrapped in a non- woven fabric material and has an elongated three-dimensional working surface with a widened front part and parallel channels, formed on at least 2/3 of the length of the core, with the parallel channels oppositely curved towards the outer part of the core at the one end, whereby the front and back ends of the core are chamfered. The thickness A is maximal in the middle part of core Zone 2, comprising Â½ of its length, and at both ends, Zone 1, comprising 1/7 of its front end and Zone 2, comprising 3/7 of its back end, the thickness is minimal.
claims:
Claims PATENT CLAIMS 1. An absorbent core for a baby diaper, consisting of a mixture of cellulose and superabsorbent, wrapped in a non-woven fabric material, characterized in that it has an elongated three-dimensional working surface with a widened front part and parallel channels, formed on at least 2/3 of the length of the core, with the parallel channels oppositely curved towards the outer part of the core at the one end, whereby the front and rear ends of the core are chamfered, the thickness A is maximal in the middle part of the core Zone 2, comprising % of its length, and at both ends, Zone 1, comprising 1/7 of the front end of its length and Zone 2, comprising 3/7 of the rear end of its length, the thickness is minimal, wherein the thickness B of the core at the beginning of Zone 1 is 40% to 60% of the thickness A and the thickness C of the core at the rear end of Zone 3 is 40% to 60% of the thickness A, with the longitudinal channels being centrally located in Zone 1 and Zone 2, whereby their curved ends are established in Zone 1. 2. An absorbent core for a baby diaper according to claim 1, characterized in that the parallel channels are not less than 2 in number with a horizontal distance between them D = 20 mm +/- 5 mm and a width of each channel E is 7 mm to 11 mm, whereby the vertical length of the core is : in Zone 1 from 350 mm to 430 mm, in Zone 2 from 180 mm to 260 mm and in Zone 3 from 60 mm to 140 mm, and the thickness A is from 3 mm to 8 mm, the thickness B is from 2 mm to 8 mm and the thickness C is from 1 mm to 4 mm, 

 while the width of the widened part is from 90 mm to 120 mm and the width of the narrowed part is from 70 mm to 110 mm. 3. An absorbent core for a baby diaper according to claims 1 and 2, characterized in that the thickness B in the central part of the core is different from the thickness A at the front end of the core and the thickness C at the rear end of the core.
